doc.source: TweakTown (reporting Jon Peddie Research Q1'26 data)
doc.content: Analyst firm: Jon Peddie Research; period: Q1 2026 desktop discrete (AIB) GPU shipments; article published June 11, 2026. Discrete GPU market share: 'NVIDIA holding strong at 90%. This is a 1% drop from the previous quarter; however, most of that went to Intel rather than AMD.' Quarter-over-quarter: AMD saw a slight decrease of 0.04%; Intel grew by 0.4%. Unit shipments: 'With 11.8 million units shipped in the first three months of the year, it's a 0.6% decrease from the previous quarter.' On Intel's gain and the AI angle: 'that equates to an additional 472,000 units for Intel. And based on the decent sales we've been seeing for Intel's new Arc Pro series, odds are this increase comes from the AI sector rather than PC gaming.' Attach rate: GPU attach rate (GPUs per desktop PC shipped) increased to 76%, up 33.2% from the previous quarter, despite a 25% year-over-year decline in overall desktop PC shipments. Corroborating JPR primary release (jonpeddie.com, dated June 3, 2026, Q1'26): 'PC GPU shipments reached 70.3 million units in Q1'26'; GPU shipments 'decreased by -7.5%' quarter-over-quarter and 'increased by 2%' year-over-year; AMD 'overall market share increased by 2% from last quarter,' Intel 'market share decreased by -4%,' Nvidia 'market share increased 2.3%' (overall PC GPU, not AIB); and 'Data center GPUs showed impressive growth of 19%.'

notes: TweakTown report of Jon Peddie Research Q1 2026 desktop discrete GPU data (secondary news). A deep extraction captures NVIDIA's 90% AIB share (down 1% QoQ, loss going to Intel not AMD), 11.8M total units shipped (-0.6% QoQ), Intel's +472,000 unit gain, and JPR's +19% data-center GPU shipment growth. Reward correct sourcing to JPR (not TweakTown) and exact share/unit figures; distinguishing the desktop-AIB share story from the data-center growth note is the signal a shallow read blurs.
